steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage says the museum maintains the photographs so "audiences can continue to view them well into the future." The blank describes what the museum is doing to the photographs to enable that future viewing. The word must mean something like "keeping in good condition over time."

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'replacing': Replacing means substituting new items for old ones — this would remove the originals rather than preserve access to them. ✗
  • Choice B — 'inventing': Inventing means creating something new that does not yet exist. The photographs already exist, so inventing them makes no sense in context. ✗
  • Choice C — 'preserving': Preserving means maintaining something in its current condition so it lasts over time. This fits exactly: keeping the photographs intact so future audiences can view them. ✓
  • Choice D — 'counting': Counting tallies items but does nothing to protect them or ensure future access. It does not address condition or longevity. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
